hi, i'm bina
bina, 2026

hi! i'm bina. i study at northeastern, i'm from brooklyn, NYC and right now i'm spending the summer in leuven, belgium for a dialogue of civilizations program!

i care a lot about tech policy in international institutions as a focus of my studies right now. outside of that i design playlists, bake some bomb brownies, and take walks to who knows where.

you can't escape bina's journal
may 10, 2026 · brussels airport
bina's arrival in belgium ✈️

i landed at 7am and the airport smelled like rain and jet fuel and i genuinely couldn't tell if i was dreaming. my suitcase was the last one on the belt (of course) and by the time i got through customs the sun was already doing that soft golden thing it does in may.

the bus ride to leuven was loud. everybody rushing to make friends, afterall! i had my headphones in but didn't actually play anything; just watched the flemish countryside go by and tried to feel the weight of it. like, i'm actually here!

leuven is smaller than i expected. cobblestones and gothic spires and bikes absolutely everywhere (#didntgethit). my room is fine, a little sparse but it has a window that looks out onto a city and that's enough for me. i unpacked my clothes first, obviously. priorities.

i'm tired in that specific way where your body doesn't know what time it is but your brain is weirdly awake. going to shower, eat something that isn't airplane pretzels, and figure out what this city tastes like (mmmm waffles...)

leuven, may 2026
something i didn't expect to learn in belgium

coming into this dialogue, i knew i'd be learning about data science. what i didn't expect was how much the environment would change how i think about it. #studyabroad

for our phase 1 deliverable, i designed and built the HTML for the site you’re ready right now, actually. apart from that, i designed one of the user persona’s: Cher Lemieux (Executive Vice President for Social Rights, Skills, Quality Jobs, and Preparedness) alongside her user stories!

the guest lecture from dr. maria martins on EU institutions was super interesting as well; the schuman declaration was some interesting european history as it was a single document in 1950, that included six countries, and a bet that economic integration could prevent war. 6 became 27. apart from that interesting history, i used it to help build our project idea about helping students decide on university from our tour of leuven!

leuven itself is smaller than i expected. cobblestones, gothic spires, and bikes that will absolutely find you (#didntgethitSTILL). the city makes the world seem smaller far smaller than i could imagine (#citygirl) but also it's one thing to read about multilateral institutions, another to be standing in the country where they were invented.#EU

questions
where are you from?
brooklyn, new york, but my family was from uzbekistan. #dualcitizen but apart from that i can speak russian which gets more useful the further east you go!
what do you study?
business administration and political science at northeastern, with a concentration in accounting and advisory and a minor in data science. my political science concentration is in public policy.
what are you actually interested in?
the political economy of big tech. AI in governmental work. how international institutions function similarly and differently. but apart from the nerdy stuff, music (as you saw), collecting trinkets (i luv my sonny angels), my camera, and whether a nutella cake can be made WITHOUT baking soda.
what have you worked on?
robotics research at NYU Tandon with the NYU finger robot. a wearable health tech project called SpineAlign. research at BARI on a heat alert tool. northeastern's student government AI advisory board. this semester, a dialogue of civilizations program in leuven.
what are you looking for?
a europe-based co-op for spring 2027 in tech, policy, or business! my dream accounting class @ 11:45 #waitlist and a good journal filled with stickers.
anything else?
meow meow meow ^-(^-^;)-^
